name: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 15.09.2025

origin.filename: Minutes of the meeting of CWWG as on 15.09.2025.pdf

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Key Insights
texts.text: Key Insights

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 1. Kharif Sowing Status: As on 12 September, 2025, the area sown under Kharif crops is 1110.80 lakh ha as compared to 1095.88 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. About 101.3 percent of the normal area has been sown under the Kharif season crops. The status of sowing under Rice, Pulses, Coarse Cereals and Sugarcane is better than in the same period of last year.
texts.text: Kharif Sowing Status: As on 12 September, 2025, the area sown under Kharif crops is 1110.80 lakh ha as compared to 1095.88 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year. About 101.3 percent of the normal area has been sown under the Kharif season crops. The status of sowing under Rice, Pulses, Coarse Cereals and Sugarcane is better than in the same period of last year.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 2. Weather Update :
texts.text: 2. Weather Update :

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Temperature: Maximum temperatures is likely to be above normal over Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, Chhattisgarh and Odisha.
texts.text: Temperature: Maximum temperatures is likely to be above normal over Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, Chhattisgarh and Odisha.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Rainfall Distribution: From June 01 -Sept 14 2025, the All-India cumulative rainfall is 7% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India:(-)20%; (ii) Northwest India: 32%; (iii) Central India: 11%; and South Peninsula: 8%.
texts.text: Rainfall Distribution: From June 01 -Sept 14 2025, the All-India cumulative rainfall is 7% higher than its Long Period Average (LPA), with regional variations: (i) East & Northeast India:(-)20%; (ii) Northwest India: 32%; (iii) Central India: 11%; and South Peninsula: 8%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The line of withdrawal of southwest monsoon passes through Sri Ganganagar, Nagaur, Jodhpur  and  Barmer.  Conditions  are  favourable  for  further  withdrawal  of  Southwest Monsoon  from  some  more  parts  of  Rajasthan  and  some  parts  of  Punjab  and  Gujarat during next 2 days.
texts.text: The line of withdrawal of southwest monsoon passes through Sri Ganganagar, Nagaur, Jodhpur  and  Barmer.  Conditions  are  favourable  for  further  withdrawal  of  Southwest Monsoon  from  some  more  parts  of  Rajasthan  and  some  parts  of  Punjab  and  Gujarat during next 2 days.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · The  Low  Pressure  area  over  north  Telangana  &  adjoining  Vidarbha  has  become  less marked on 15 th  September, 2025. However, the associated cyclonic circulation persisted over  the  same  region  and  extended  up  to  4.5  km  above  mean  sea  level  tilting southwestwards with height.
texts.text: The  Low  Pressure  area  over  north  Telangana  &  adjoining  Vidarbha  has  become  less marked on 15 th  September, 2025. However, the associated cyclonic circulation persisted over  the  same  region  and  extended  up  to  4.5  km  above  mean  sea  level  tilting southwestwards with height.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Heavy to Very heavy rainfall over northeastern states and Maharashtra for next 3 days.
texts.text: Heavy to Very heavy rainfall over northeastern states and Maharashtra for next 3 days.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Scattered  to  fairly  widespread  rainfall  likely  over  Western  India.  Isolated  to  scattered rainfall likely over the rest parts of the country.
texts.text: Scattered  to  fairly  widespread  rainfall  likely  over  Western  India.  Isolated  to  scattered rainfall likely over the rest parts of the country.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: District-wise rainfall distribution of States is attached in Annexure I.
texts.text: District-wise rainfall distribution of States is attached in Annexure I.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: 3. Inputs Situation
texts.text: 3. Inputs Situation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: a) Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  11 September  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  161 important  reservoirs  across  the  country  (monitored  by  CWC)  is  158.765  billion  cubic meter,  which  is  87%  of  total  live  storage  capacity  (182.479  BCM).  The  current  year's storage  is  nearly  102.5  percent  of  last  year's  storage  and  114.8  percent  of  the  average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.
texts.text: Water  Reservoir  Status :  As  on  11 September  2025,  the  total  live  water  storage  in  161 important  reservoirs  across  the  country  (monitored  by  CWC)  is  158.765  billion  cubic meter,  which  is  87%  of  total  live  storage  capacity  (182.479  BCM).  The  current  year's storage  is  nearly  102.5  percent  of  last  year's  storage  and  114.8  percent  of  the  average storage of the last ten years during the corresponding period. The overall storage position is better than the corresponding period of last year in the country as a whole and is also better than the Normal storage during the corresponding period.

texts.label: page_footer
texts.orig: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare
texts.text: Agricultural Statistics Division| Department of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare |Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers Welfare

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: b) Groundwater Levels : About 43.1% of the total monitoring wells show depth to water level in the range of 0 to 5 m below ground level and 37.6% show depth to water level in the range of 5 to 10 m below ground level. About 19.2% of the total monitoring wells show water  level  deeper  than  10  m  below  ground  level.  The  groundwater  level  fluctuation analysis of Pre-monsoon 2025 with decadal mean of Pre-monsoon (2015-2024) indicates that 67.7% of analysed wells are showing rise and 33.0% are showing fall in water level. Remaining 0.3% stations analysed do not show any change in water level.
texts.text: Groundwater Levels : About 43.1% of the total monitoring wells show depth to water level in the range of 0 to 5 m below ground level and 37.6% show depth to water level in the range of 5 to 10 m below ground level. About 19.2% of the total monitoring wells show water  level  deeper  than  10  m  below  ground  level.  The  groundwater  level  fluctuation analysis of Pre-monsoon 2025 with decadal mean of Pre-monsoon (2015-2024) indicates that 67.7% of analysed wells are showing rise and 33.0% are showing fall in water level. Remaining 0.3% stations analysed do not show any change in water level.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: As  per  the  Ground  Water  Drought  Indices  (GWDI),  in  pre-monsoon  2025,  90%  of  the analysed  wells  are  in  the  normal  category,  while  10%  of  the  analysed  wells  are  in  the drought category.
texts.text: As  per  the  Ground  Water  Drought  Indices  (GWDI),  in  pre-monsoon  2025,  90%  of  the analysed  wells  are  in  the  normal  category,  while  10%  of  the  analysed  wells  are  in  the drought category.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: c) Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.
texts.text: Seeds  and  Fertilizers :  For  Kharif  2025,  certified  seed  availability  is  178.64  lakh  quintals against a requirement of 164.05 lakh quintals, ensuring a surplus of 14.60 lakh quintals. The fertilizer supply remains stable across all products.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: 4. Pests & Diseases : Intensity of pests and diseases at some fields has been reported to be above Economic Threshold Level for Soyabean (Girdle Beetle & Tobacco Caterpillar) in Maharashtra & Soyabean (Variety RBS 2001-4) (Soyabean Yellow Mosaic Virus)) in Madhya Pradesh and for Paddy (US 312) (Caseworm)) in Jharkhand. The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring is being carried out.
texts.text: Pests & Diseases : Intensity of pests and diseases at some fields has been reported to be above Economic Threshold Level for Soyabean (Girdle Beetle & Tobacco Caterpillar) in Maharashtra & Soyabean (Variety RBS 2001-4) (Soyabean Yellow Mosaic Virus)) in Madhya Pradesh and for Paddy (US 312) (Caseworm)) in Jharkhand. The Advisory has been issued by the survey team to the farms and regular monitoring is being carried out.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · During the week (04 Sept -10 Sept 2025), equal to LPA rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 0%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st June to 10 th Sept 2025) is higher than LPA by 8%.
texts.text: During the week (04 Sept -10 Sept 2025), equal to LPA rainfall was observed, as compared to Long Period Average (LPA) by 0%. Cumulative seasonal rainfall (01 st June to 10 th Sept 2025) is higher than LPA by 8%.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Conditions are likely  to  become  favourable  for  withdrawal  of  Southwest  Monsoon  from some parts of northwest India towards end of the week.
texts.text: Conditions are likely  to  become  favourable  for  withdrawal  of  Southwest  Monsoon  from some parts of northwest India towards end of the week.

texts.text: Overall, rainfall activity is likely to be normal to above normal over northwest & central India  and  many  parts  of  south  Peninsular  India;  near  normal  over  most  parts  of  east  & northeast India during the week.

texts.text: Soil Moisture Observation

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Soil moisture as on 05 Sept - 11 Sept 2025 was similar/better than the average of the past 9 years  in  major  parts  of  M.P,  Rajasthan,  Uttar  Pradesh,  Chhattisgarh,  Gujarat,  Jharkhand, West  Bengal,  major  parts  of  Odisha,  Haryana,  Punjab,  Kerala,  Karnataka,  Maharashtra, Telangana, A.P. and southern parts of Bihar.
texts.text: Soil moisture as on 05 Sept - 11 Sept 2025 was similar/better than the average of the past 9 years  in  major  parts  of  M.P,  Rajasthan,  Uttar  Pradesh,  Chhattisgarh,  Gujarat,  Jharkhand, West  Bengal,  major  parts  of  Odisha,  Haryana,  Punjab,  Kerala,  Karnataka,  Maharashtra, Telangana, A.P. and southern parts of Bihar.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · It was lower than average of past 9 years in northern parts of Bihar , in  pockets of eastern part of UP, in parts of Assam, in pockets southern parts of Odisha and in parts of Tamil Nadu.
texts.text: It was lower than average of past 9 years in northern parts of Bihar , in  pockets of eastern part of UP, in parts of Assam, in pockets southern parts of Odisha and in parts of Tamil Nadu.

texts.label: caption

texts.text: Flood  Water  Inundation  analysis  in  Punjab  : Major  impacted  districts  are  Gurdaspur, Firozpur, Amritsar, Kapurthala, Tarn Taran, Fazilka.

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Kharif Crops - Area coverage
texts.text: Kharif Crops - Area coverage

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Rice :  About  438.51  lakh  ha  (108.0%  of  Normal)  area  coverage  under  Kharif  Rice  as compared to 430.06 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Rice :  About  438.51  lakh  ha  (108.0%  of  Normal)  area  coverage  under  Kharif  Rice  as compared to 430.06 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Maize :  About  94.84  lakh  ha  (120.1%  of  Normal)  area  coverage  under  Maize  has  been reported as compared to 84.30 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Maize :  About  94.84  lakh  ha  (120.1%  of  Normal)  area  coverage  under  Maize  has  been reported as compared to 84.30 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Moong : About 34.54 lakh ha (96.8% of Normal) area coverage under Moong as compared to 34.86 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Moong : About 34.54 lakh ha (96.8% of Normal) area coverage under Moong as compared to 34.86 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Soybean : About 120.43 lakh ha (94.7% of Normal) area coverage under Soyabean has been reported  as  compared  to  126.24  lakh  ha  during  the  corresponding  period  of  last  year. Soyabean area diverted in Maize in MP and Maharashtra.
texts.text: Soybean : About 120.43 lakh ha (94.7% of Normal) area coverage under Soyabean has been reported  as  compared  to  126.24  lakh  ha  during  the  corresponding  period  of  last  year. Soyabean area diverted in Maize in MP and Maharashtra.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Sugarcane :  About  57.31  lakh  ha  area  coverage  under  Sugarcane  has  been  reported  as compared to 55.68 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.
texts.text: Sugarcane :  About  57.31  lakh  ha  area  coverage  under  Sugarcane  has  been  reported  as compared to 55.68 lakh ha during the corresponding period of last year.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Cotton : About 109.64 lakh ha (84.7% of Normal) area coverage has been reported compared to  112.48  lakh  ha  during  the  corresponding  period  of  last  year.  Cotton  area  diverted  in Paddy and Groundnut respectively in Punjab & Haryana and Gujarat.
texts.text: Cotton : About 109.64 lakh ha (84.7% of Normal) area coverage has been reported compared to  112.48  lakh  ha  during  the  corresponding  period  of  last  year.  Cotton  area  diverted  in Paddy and Groundnut respectively in Punjab & Haryana and Gujarat.

texts.text: Availability  of  fertilizer  has  been  found  comfortable.  Details  of  Requirement,  Receipt, Availability, Sales and Stock position of Major Fertilizers (Urea, DAP, MOP, Complex and SSP) during Kharif 2025 as on 12.09.2025 is given below:
